segments.text: Samsung once launched a TV box that was
actually furniture. It sounded like a
gimmick, but it was actually a genius
solution for a massive problem. They
realized millions of huge TV boxes were
ending up in landfills. So, they printed
a strange dot matrix pattern on all of
them. On its own, the pattern was
looking like a printing error. It meant
[music] nothing, but it was actually a
blueprint. Owners could scan the QR
code, connect the dots with a pencil,
and slice the box into specific shapes.
It was basically a cardboard Lego. But
did anyone use it, or was it just
marketing? It worked. People built
magazine racks, shelves, and even
catouses. It proved that with the right
design, your trash does not have to be
trash. For more innovative content,
subscribe.
